The renovation was the real upgrade move: every seat in the bowl was replaced for better comfort, and the concourses were widened with more food-and-beverage options including grab-and-go, so long nights feel easier. And the Uptown location at 333 E Trade St is the endgame advantagestep outside and youre immediately in walking distance of hotels, restaurants, and the rest of the citys energy.

Saw NIN at the Spectrum Center last night, the show was great however the venue is getting increasingly more difficult to visit. Security is jerky and defensive in general, I've seen many bands at the Spectrum Center going all the way back to when it was still Time Warner. I frequently go to concerts and am well versed in venuearena etiquette. That being said, a few years back I purchased a LIVE NATION concert bag, which is basically a small clear rucksack, at a concert in which my already small bag was still too big. Since then, I have taken this 'concert bag' to every show I've been to including ones at the Spectrum Center because it is literally designed to meet venue bag requirements. Last night however, security tried to keep me from entering with my 'concert bag'. The measuring board they were using for bag sizing was 2 centimeters shorter than my bag that was literally designed and sold by the people that sold me the tickets to this concert!!!!! Like... excuse me?!?! I finally convinced them to let me in with it BUT after that I had a Zippo lighter in my bag and they wanted me to throw it away!!!! My concert companion ran it back to the hotel but seriously?!?! Your staff asked me to throw away a 50 lighter!!! One even said 'if you throw that away I am gonna take it'. Evidently they weren't allowing anyone inside with lighters, vapes or even a pack of cigarettes!!! What?!? I understand safety and security rules and regulations, most of which I think are pretty reasonable and it is reassuring no one is allowed in with a knife or weapon BUT what is someone going to do with a cigarette? Are you worried someone is going to set a fire? It is so dumb and the security situation in general is getting ever more draconian. If you're going to be so strict with this type of stuff you need to have a bagcoat check so you don't have to throw away your personal property. How anyone thinks it is reasonable to ask a patron to throw their baglighterwhatever away is beyond me. I will say the lovely girl that scanned our tickets was very pleasant and sweet. The best part of the venue is the fountain Diet Dr. Pepper and free refills, with the price of drinks the refills should be free.

We visited the Spectrum Center to watch the Hornets take on Detroit and had a great experience overall. Getting there was super easy and we found parking just across the street for 20, which honestly felt like a steal. Coming from New England, that's unheard of. The arena itself is a great place to catch a basketball game. The walkways are bright, clean, and spacious, and the seating area was the same. I had plenty of room in my seat with a few extra inches of leg room, which I never get at 6'2". We were sitting in the lower bowl so it may be different higher up, but our experience was very comfortable. The staff throughout the arena were all friendly and welcoming, which added to the overall atmosphere and made the whole experience feel that much better. The one downside was the food. The premade chicken nachos and tacos were a miss. The chicken didn't taste like chicken and we're pretty sure it came from a can. That said, some of the other food options looked much better, so we'd just choose differently next time. Overall the Spectrum Center is a fantastic venue. Easy access, affordable parking, friendly staff, a clean and comfortable atmosphere, and a fun night out. Just skip the premade chicken items and you're golden.Seat comfortComfortable

The arena itself? Its nice. Getting in? Its like Fort Knox. Its worse than your favorite major airport. If youre driving from say, two hours away, after a day of work, and you expect to be in your seat by the 7pm Hornets start time 7:30 or 8:00 being the usual NBA weeknight standard, youre sorely mistaken. Twice I have spent more than I ever have on tickets, hundreds of dollars, to sit at floor level for Hornets vs. Knicks, and twice I have been on time but missed the first quarter. Fool me once, shame on you; fool me twice, shame on me. The queue to get in is absolutely ridiculous. I havent seen anything like this to get into any arena anywhere. The process was just as disorderly both times. Both times I was separated from my party to go through a different line, single file, yet theyre yelling at us to have our tickets out and ready to go, not really taking into account that one of us might be holding all of the tickets? Then once you go through several metal detectors and, if youre lucky like me, a thorough hands-on inspection usually involving scrutiny of my bottle opener on my keychain, youre almost through the first quarter and youve wasted 14 of the youve shelled out to sit close to the court. One of the members of my party is blind in one eye and going through cancer treatment. There was no regard for her being separated from me during the asinine entrance process; in fact, just the opposite from the belligerent staff mindlessly barking orders at hundreds of fans missing the first part of the game. I gave it one more shot. Shame on me, like I said. From now on, I will drive to Atlanta. Its worth the extra time in the car to be able to get in without chaos and see the entire game. Besides, the Hornets are perpetually terrible and seem to have no direction regardless of coach or talent. I do not like the Hawks, but at least theyre decent. I guess at least Spectrum is being consistent, as their venue is just as abhorrent as their home internet service.

If youre coming from out of town do not bring cigaretteslighter or a small purse. Waited in line for 30 minutes to get in and then told we have to stand in another line. Missed the whole first quarter. Nasty workers all around. GET THERE EARLY IF PLANNING TO GO TO ANY EVEN AT THE CHARLOTTE SPECTRUM CENTER

Good: Nice Arena, good food, good operations at food stands, plenty of seating, lively and fun hornets games. BAD: I wasn't pleased that the older lady running security through metal detectors pulled a line full of black people, she also separated my from my husband and sent him through the detectors. Felt like profiling. Nobody in the detector line was another race.

My recent visit to the Spectrum Center in Charlotte left me with mixed feelings. While the venue itself boasts many positive attributes, a significant issue with policy enforcement severely detracted from the experience. Facility and Staff 55 Stars On the positive side, the facility was exceptionally clean, well-maintained, and modern. Navigating the arena was easy, and the overall atmosphere, aside from the smoke, felt welcoming. The staff deserve high praise; every interaction I had with an employee was friendly, professional, and efficient. It's clear that the Spectrum Center team prioritizes guest service and cleanliness. The Smoking Problem 15 Stars Unfortunately, the experience was made deeply unpleasant due to the pervasive smell and presence of marijuana smoke. Despite the Spectrum Center being a smoke-free venue with clear policies prohibiting all smoking products including vapes and e-cigarettes, this rule was clearly not being adequately enforced. The odor was noticeable throughout the seating area, making the air quality poor and causing discomfort. For guests who are sensitive to smoke, this lack of enforcement creates a major barrier to enjoying an event. While the staff were friendly, I believe management needs to significantly step up security and usher training to ensure their smoke-free policy is actually upheld, particularly for substances like cannabis which are still restricted in the venue.

Bargatze show 112025. Close seats second row above court. Sound was awful. Reverberation, distortion inaudible material from performers. Left my seat and went from lobby to higher seats until sound was acceptable. Expensive seats were a waste of money.
My husband walks with a walker, and EVERYONE we came in contact with went above and beyond to assist us!

The Spectrum Center is a great venue. The only thing to brace yourself for is parking - expect to pay around 40. Thats more of an uptown problem than a Spectrum Center problem, but the nearby lots definitely raise their prices on event days. Inside, everything was solid. The facility looked updated and well cared for, the bathrooms were clean, and the staff was genuinely kind and helpful. Plenty of space, plenty of food options including Chick-fil-A, and no weird smells anywhere. Ill definitely be back for another event. Shoutout to the Hornets.
I always love the spectrum. Thats the reason why we travel from Virginia to Charlotte for events. Close to the hotel and we can walk everywhere.

I attend a lot of concerts around Charlotte NC and this is by far the worst place I've attended. I went to the Brandy Monica concert last night Thursday 111325 and paid 200 for floor seats. They had Chalk on the ground marking the rowsseats and the actual seats were extremely close together. I was so uncomfortable the entire time. You're literally touching both of your neighbors arms the entire night. When I tried to stand up and sing, I noticed everyone had their hands together in front of them because it wasn't any room to allow your hands to hand beside your body if someone was sitting next to you. You couldn't dance or sway side to side without hitting someone. I'm very small, 140 Lbs 5'4 in height, so it's no way I should have felt squeezed when both of my neighbors were small in size also. I texted a couple of other people who had regular seats in the 100 and 200 section and they both said their seats were uncomfortable and tight also. The traffic getting to the location was horrible which I expected but not worth it when arriving at this place. I live in Rock Hill SC and I left at 6PM and arrived at 7:14 from the bad traffic which is normally a 30 minute drive. Clearly this establishment is only trying to money gauge and not consider those paying to attend. The only good thing here was their employees were very nice. Alcoholic drinks are the regular concert price 16-28 and I didnt purchase food because it's too compacted of a space to comfortably eat in your seat. I sat on the floor row M and seat 4 and it was the worst!!! Everyone around me was complaining. I ended up leaving early and missing the most important part of the concert which I've never ever done in my life. I'm very patient but this was too much. I would not recommend this place and I would not return, even if it was free for my favorite person. Just ridiculous.

This place allowed fans who camped days in advance to run the line for the Billie Eilish concert. They did not allow people to get their official wristband for GA floor unless they had a fan made wristband. This was never advertised on their website or anywhere, so this was incredibly unfair to those who did not know of this. It was extremely chaotic and a lot of people were upset, and the security were not budging. They followed these random peoples rules. They state that they dont encourage camping, but they were making sure that campers that were their days in advance had snacks. Completely unfair and should be held accountable.
Amazing staff and so helpful!

Allowing a random unqualified fan A NON EMPLOYEE to dictate the order in which general admission wristbands were distributed is highly unprofessional. It should not have been the responsibility of an unaffiliated individual to manage wristbands or verify line order. We witnessed venue staff consulting her personal phone list and deferring to her judgment multiple times. She was not part of your team, and its concerning that her system was treated as official. Especially when she was being dishonest about distributing the bracelets in a first come first serve basis. She saved bands for her friends. Gave better numbers to people she liked and worse numbers to people she disliked. The people in the beginning of line got their places in line and were able to leave and go to concerts in Raleigh and come back. Etc!! Absolutely nothing posted about general admission on Spectrum Centers website and social media pages were actually followed. I wouldnt trust this venue to abide by anything they say after this weekend. It says line up at 5th street north entrance day of show The fan created line was started on N Brevard St. the day of the show and Spectrum employees crossed the street OFF SPECTRUM PROPERTY to distribute bands in accordance to this random fan girls jurisdiction following her list on her phone to a tee. The line of people on 5th street at 8am were told they would have to go to the back of the line a whole street over even though they followed instructions posted by venue. Show GA ticket for wristband -they made us show the FAN MADE wristband to them in addition to the ticket to get a wristband. Line up starts at 3pm in wristband order with final check in at 4pm. Guests arriving after 4pm will line up after the last wristband holder we arrived at 3pm and no effort was made night 1 to put people in order until 5:00. On N2, a couple of announcements were made to put ourselves in order but no actual effort from spectrum employees in enforcing order was done until 5:20 and even that was very minimal. No one was put in the back of the line for arriving after 4:00 in fact people coming in at 3-4pm were even being told they could leave and be back at 5:30. Again, extremely unprofessional making people who followed the rules sit there for 2.5 hours for absolutely no reason. And then creating mass panic at 5-5:30 to get in the correct order when everyone is already anxious. Overnight camping is not permitted at Spectrum Center or surrounding parking lots While the camping that took place was across the street of the property, Spectrum Center management went out of their way to reward camping by bringing gift boxes to the campers there earlier in the week and crossed the street OFF spectrum property to prioritize the campers for wristband distribution. They made no effort to discourage the act of camping in a dangerous area 5 days before the concert.

I was stung by a bee in line for general admission at 5:00 Monday and was told the venue could not give me anything for it until I was in the building due to liability concerns and I would have to wait until we were inside then leave my spot on barricade to go to the medic room. I asked if I could at least go wash my hand where the sting was and they directed me to the hotel across the street where JUSTIN said to go for restrooms. The hotel wouldnt let me in and said they made their restroom closed to the public. I understand not giving me medication but not even an ice pack or water to wash my hand or access to restroom is awful. Terrible customer service. Employees werent even remotely kind about this situation as well.

I waited to post this until the concerts were over since the girls that the Spectrum Center let dictate the line were so petty and rude and didnt want them to take it out on me like they were on others. Obviously the ones posting in this thanking the Spectrum Center were involved in the whole process and of course theyd thank them for it, it worked out perfectly in their favor, without regard to anything else. This entire situation was extremely unprofessional. Honestly, legal trouble too. These girls were not employees, and the Spectrum Center walking off of property to hand out wristbands to honor these girls who were holding wristbands for their friends, threatening to remove others from their fan made lists, and going to the Raleigh concert while keeping their place in line in Charlotte is insane. Justin and Daniel were being borderline flirtatious with these girls as well, and you wont get anywhere going to their email since Justin responds back to those emails. Not only was this wildly inappropriate and unprofessional, these girls were laughing in peoples faces, making fun of people, taking turns going to their hotel but then threatening to remove people from their list who werent at their tents the whole time, etc. This is why you do not honor fan made lines and why no where doesnt 99.9 of the time. Its dishonest and up for debate on what is truly fact. Not to mention, you all wouldnt let unhoused people camp out the way everyone did for these shows, and that speaks volumes as well. I hate that I had to be part of this camping problem, because you all let this go down and the only way to get a good spot was to comply with these mean girls who tried to act like they were nice, calm, and professional when people were obviously upset with their behavior. Its also very obvious by you all giving them candy and stuff to post on tik tok is just a ploy for your new arena announcement, its pretty disturbing. Theres better ways to do this, and you all should take some serious pointers from the Heritage Center in Cincinnati. When we went there almost a year ago, they handed out wristbands a day prior to the concert but didnt say what exact time until like an hour before and were partnered with the police to escort campers away that were on and near the property until it was closer to time to hand them out. Once you got your wristbands at Heritage, you got a set time that matched your wristband number to come back the day of the concert to help line people up prior, unlike this venue waiting until exactly 5:30 to actually like people up. Heritage also had Porta Potties for people, water bottles, ice packs etc. This was a huge fumble on your alls part and disgraceful. I hate I had to be part of the camping issue due to your alls negligence and willingness to actually step in and handle the wristbands. Honestly though, the issue wasnt the camping, it was you all allowing random fans to dictate the whole entire general admission line rather than the actual arena. You all need to get a grip.

THEY HONORED FAN MADE WRISTBANDS FOR A BILLIE CONCERT AND VIOLATED THEIR POLICIES AND WERE DISGUSTING AND RUDE TO THE PEOPLE WHO CAME AT 4AM AS SPECTRUM EMPLOYEES DIRECTED AND KICKED THEM OFF THE PROPERTY BUT THE PEOPLE WITH AMAZON WRISTBANDS GOT REAL WRISTBANDS FIRST. Attending the recent NBA game at the Spectrum Center was an electrifying experience that I won't soon forget. From the moment I stepped into the arena, the atmosphere was buzzing with excitement and anticipation. The energy from the crowd was palpable, with fans decked out in their favorite team jerseys and ready to cheer their hearts out. The Spectrum Center itself is a fantastic venue with excellent sightlines and comfortable seating. Whether you're sitting close to the court or up in the nosebleeds, you can still feel connected to the action. The modern amenities, including big screens for replays and highlights, made every moment of the game even more thrilling. As the players took to the court, the intensity of the matchup was evident. The talent on display was astounding, showcasing some of the best players in the league. Every dunk, three-pointer, and pivotal play was met with roars from the crowd, creating a sense of unity among the fans. I found myself caught up in the emotion of the game, celebrating each basket and holding my breath during tense moments. The halftime show was also a highlight, featuring impressive performances that kept the energy high. It was a great way to break up the action and keep everyone entertained. Overall, my experience at the Spectrum Center during the NBA game was unforgettable. The combination of an exhilarating atmosphere, top-tier basketball, and engaged fans made it an incredible night out. I can't wait to return for another game soon!

Every single thing should have been fantastic- entry was easy, staff was communicative and polite, signage was on point and I could find everything I wanted. Ushers were easy to find, helpful, and lovely. I would have had a genuinely amazing experience here- except there was a giant spot light pointed directly at my seat at a very dark show. I dont turn on the over head lights in my house almost ever. Its like someone screaming in my ear. Its abrasive, its horrible, its impossible to ignore. This was exponentially worse than a home overhead light- it was an actual spotlight! In a dark concert! I also do not like being the center of attention, so knowing that my every move is highlighted to disturb everyone around me completely takes any enjoyment of the show Im watching. I had wanted to see two of the bands playing for decades. I was so excited to see them, and though my seats werent good, it cost me a lot to be there. We drove three hours to get to the show. I asked an usher for help- though polite, his answer was essentially its sold out, get over it. I also work at a performing arts venue- this is unacceptable. At no point when I booked was I alerted that there would be a spot light on my seat. We ended up having to leave the show while the main band was still on because I had a migraine from this light. Please be aware if you book an aisle seat, you might have a spot light uncomfortably on you. Probably just see whatever act you want to see in a different city and dont go to this venue.

Went for a Hornets game recently and man this place is great. No wait to enter with prepaid tickets, we walked right up to the front and briefly displayed our tickets and were on our way. Plenty of police presence, felt very safe the whole event and before and after which is great considering the crime rate in Charlotte. I personally saw no issues with fans of either team in attendance, and I wouldnt hesitate to revisit the Spectrum Center at anytime for any event, it was a wonderful experience from start to finish.

Spectrum Center Ignores Accessibility Concerns Customer Complaints Even at the Highest Levels My family and I attended WWE Monday Night Raw at Spectrum Center, and it was one of the worst venue experiences weve ever had. Despite spending over 800 on tickets, taking time off work and school, and traveling for the event, we were met with zero empathy or assistance from the staff. Key Issues We Faced: I have a serious medical condition and a severe fear of heights, yet our seats were in a high, exposed section. I asked over 10 employees for help before being sent to guest relations, where I was ignored for more than 10 minutes. I was told no accommodations were available, despite offering to pay for an upgrade. I later learned this was false, as another family was moved for free. I was forced to spend an additional 1,500 just to sit in a safe space with my family. A staff member pulled a curtain shut on my crying 5- and 7-year-old children, treating us like we didnt belong. Spectrum Center Also Fails Disabled Guests in Parking Accessibility: They do NOT offer handicap parking and do not own their own parking facility. If you require accessible entry, you must be dropped off at a designated areaso plan ahead, because they make it as difficult as possible. Spectrum Center Leadership Ignores Complaints Even When Contacted Directly I have sent multiple emails to every senior leader at Spectrum Center, including C-suite executives, and have been completely ignored. I even paid to send LinkedIn direct messages, which were read but never responded to. Here are the senior executives I contacted who refused to acknowledge my concerns: Rick Schnall Co-Chairman Governor Gabe Plotkin Co-Chairman Alternate Governor Michael Jordan Alternate Governor Dave Novak Executive Committee Member Rotating Alternate Governor Chris Shumway Executive Committee Member Rotating Alternate Governor Shelly Cayette-Weston President of Business Operations James R. Jordan Chief Operating Officer Seth Bennett Chief Marketing Officer Despite their high-ranking positions, not one of them has responded to my repeated outreach. Bottom Line: Spectrum Centers customer service is horrible, misleading, and completely fails people with medical needs. If youre considering going to an event hereespecially if you or a loved one has a disabilityBEWARE. SpectrumCenter CharlotteNC WWE CustomerServiceFail DisabilityRights DoBetter AccessibilityMatters IgnoredByLeadership

Attending a concert at the Spectrum Center turned out to be a disappointing experience. First, parking options were inconvenient, forcing me to take the light rail, which felt unsafe and out of place for a night where I dressed up for the occasion. Inside, the concessions were a mess. The first stand I approached only had some strange nacho creation, and when I asked for simple nachos, they said it would cost the same. I ended up paying 50 for food I didnt even want, only to later discover other stands offering what I was looking forregular nachos and popcorn from an actual machine. It felt like a scam to place the worst options up front. On top of that, despite arriving early, my experience was ruined by inconsiderate latecomers. People streamed in over an hour into the show, forcing our entire row to stand up repeatedly. My view was obstructed over a dozen times, completely disrupting the performance I paid to see. For a venue hosting large-scale events, I expected better organization, safer transportation options, and a more thoughtful setup for concessions and seating policies. I wont be rushing back anytime soon.

We have been going to the spectrum Center for years. I was previously a partial season ticket holder for the Charlotte Hornets. I gave them up due to the expenses that come with traveling to Charlotte from Winston Salem. My kids took me yesterday for the Knicks game as a Birthday gift. After being on an u organized and chaotic line for half hour, we get to the front and a security lady comes out of nowhere and tells my wife she cant go in with her CLEAR bag. I advised her we purchased it the previous year in the arena. She said, New owners, new rules in a nasty tone. The bag was about 3 inches bigger than the 7 they now admit, Mind you, its clear and was only 14 full. Then her and another security guard said get out of the line and go to the back after you put your bag in the car. They were doing this to a lot of people, even one with a stroller. They were not professional at all. Super Nasty! The worse part was that inside we saw bigger SOLID pocketbooks and bags!!!! Luckily it was a good game and I cooled down. The new colors on the basketball floor are cool. The overhead is crystal clear. Staff inside was nice. Prices are still high for food and drinks, add tickets and parking and its becoming a rich mans night out. We had fun and our NY Knicks won! Update: Its nice to come out here once in a while to catch a game but it gets expensive. Between tickets and parking, its become a rich mans sport, just like every other sport. My biggest complain is the food quality. They have switched over to self serve kiosks where you tap your card and grab and go. The food does not feel fresh and could be sitting quite a bit of time. They also have someone making you take the cap off your drinks and throwing them out. This becomes a hassle to carry and spill and sodas go flat quickly. We got the Chicken strips and waffle fries and they were worse than what you would get at a gas station. The nachos come in a small bag with a sealed cold cheese sauce, just nasty! Not a lot of nachos in the bag and no option of jalapeños or chili or HOT cheese! Just not worth it for the money you pay. Better off eating ahead or after the games. I would love to see more affordable t shirts or value worthy gifts for everyone, not just the people with money to spare. Would love to come to more games but 1 or 2 a year is my limit. I can only afford nosebleeds and for that I might as well stay in the comfort of my home.

The Spectrum Center in Charlotte, NC, is a well-cared-for facility with impressive amenities and modern features. The venue itself is great, offering a comfortable and enjoyable experience for various events. However, the overall environment can be disappointing due to the crowds it attracts. Unfortunately, this often leads to the area becoming trashy and dirty, and potentially dangerous detracting from the otherwise excellent facilities. While the Spectrum Center has potential, the issues with ambiance and the behavior of some attendees impact the overall experience. Improved crowd management and maintenance could make it a superior venue.

Recently, I drove seven hours to take my son to the Spectrum Center in Charlotte, and we had an amazing time. The arena lived up to our expectations, delivering a memorable experience. The only downside was the lack of pre-event activities outside the arena, but the attentive staff more than made up for it by keeping us engaged. We opted for seats in the Founder area, specifically the Flight Deck, and it was well worth it. For just 40 each, we enjoyed access to a buffet and our own table, all while being entertained. The only suggestion would be to have a few extra servers at the stairs for smoother service, but it didn't hinder our enjoyment. To enhance others' experiences, I've included some pictures since there wasn't much information available about the seats beforehand. Overall, a fantastic outing kudos to the Spectrum Center team for a job well done.

Went for the NCAA Tournament. Sat in the upper deck and suites. 200 level : This level has a basic feel compared to downstairs. There is a bar that is decent but the speakers were blown and everyone could hear it. Beware that the upper seats are so high the game feels like its in another state. Good flow in and out of the upper deck. Luxury suites: Amenities are low but the suite features are better. Seems to be renovated and clean. Liquor is bottle service only when we were there. Food quantity is very low compared to the price. There was also a critical POS system issue that cause multiple charges to my card. Tons of holds. Server was helpful and we tipped accordingly. Overall, Spectrum center is a decent arena. Easy to get to and easy out. Food could improve significantly in the basic food stands. Food quantity could be stated in the suites menus. Its very vague and thats probably on purpose. We will do the suites moving forward to be closer to the court.

We were at the Spectrum Center for the NCAA Mens basketball games. Weve traveled to different venues for 25 years all over the country and weve never seen such clean bathrooms and facilities. Over the course of the two days of games, we watched the crews constantly cleaning, picking up trash and checking the restrooms which were immaculate! Job well done to the cleaning crews!

Spectrum Center - The home of Charlotte Hornets. I have been there many times for different games. It was always a great experience though.
